codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the expected behavior: CI failure analysis, root cause identification, and automated fix proposal/application. All 7 tests pass. Code is clean with minor issues.

issues.description: In `apply_fix()` for `FixType::AddImport`, the import insertion does not check for existing imports. This could lead to duplicate imports if the same import is already present in the file.
Confusing string handling: `extract_missing_import()` returns strings like `use crate::client::HttpClient;` (with semicolon), then `trim_end_matches(';')` removes it, then `format!(
, ...)` re-adds it. Works but readability could be improved.
The `fix` operation takes up to `max_retries` fixes (`.take(max_retries)`), not retries failed fixes `max_retries` times. This differs from `auto_heal` semantics and may not match expectations.
Plain text file location extraction (`extract_file_location()`) is pattern-sensitive and may miss errors in non-standard formats. Works for common Rust compiler output but could miss edge cases.

summary: Review passed. The self-healing CI tool implementation fully satisfies the feature requirements: CI failure analysis (JSON and plain text parsing), root cause identification (grouping by file and categorizing failures), and automated fix proposal and application. Tests pass (7/7), build succeeds, and clippy passes. Minor non-blocking issues noted around duplicate import prevention, string handling clarity, and retry semantics documentation.
